title: Nardosmia laevigata
taxonomy: Nardosmia laevigata is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant is an annual hemiparasitic herb, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing the roots of other plants. Nardosmia laevigata was previously classified in the family Scrophulariaceae. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Orobanchaceae family.

intro: Nardosmia laevigata, also known as Lignaria laevigata, is a plant species belonging to the family Calyceraceae, native to the mountainous regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Nardosmia laevigata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Nardosmia laevigata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
